channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: China test-launches a ballistic missile in the South Pacific and raises regional concerns
item.description: The launch, using a dummy warhead, took place the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defense treaty meant to counter Chinese influence in the Pacific.
item.content:encoded:
The launch, using a dummy warhead, took place the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defense treaty meant to counter Chinese influence in the Pacific.

(Image credit: Li Xiangchao)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Tanker set ablaze after being struck by projectile in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iranian state television said the tanker came under attack after ignoring warnings but did not directly claim the assault.
item.content:encoded:
Iranian state television said the tanker came under attack after ignoring warnings but did not directly claim the assault.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war escalates before NATO summit
item.description: Russia pounds Kyiv ahead of NATO summit, as Ukraine war returns to center stage amid high-stakes diplomacy.
item.content:encoded: Russia pounds Kyiv ahead of NATO summit, as Ukraine war returns to center stage amid high-stakes diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: Trump, Mamdani give contrasting July 4th speeches. And, U.S. faces Belgium in World Cup
item.description: For the nation's 250th birthday, President Trump and NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani had opposing visions of America. And, the U.S. and Belgium face off today in the FIFA World Cup.
item.content:encoded:
For the nation's 250th birthday, President Trump and NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani had opposing visions of America. And, the U.S. and Belgium face off today in the FIFA World Cup.

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Huge crowds of mourners join a funeral procession for Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
item.description: Iran holds a funeral procession for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei more than four months after he was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes.
item.content:encoded:
Iran holds a funeral procession for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei more than four months after he was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: Russia's missile and drone attacks on Ukraine kill at least 22
item.description: Russia unleashed waves of missiles and drones at Ukraine, killing at least 22 people. The attacks exposed gaps in the country's air defenses more than four years into Moscow's full-scale invasion.
item.content:encoded:
Russia unleashed waves of missiles and drones at Ukraine, killing at least 22 people. The attacks exposed gaps in the country's air defenses more than four years into Moscow's full-scale invasion.

(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump won spending promises from NATO last year. This week, he'll try to enforce them
item.description: President Donald Trump is heading to Ankara, Turkey, for the annual NATO summit. Last year, he pushed NATO allies to spend more on defense. This year, his mission is to try to enforce those pledges.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump is heading to Ankara, Turkey, for the annual NATO summit. Last year, he pushed NATO allies to spend more on defense. This year, his mission is to try to enforce those pledges.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Y si sí: the phrase uniting Mexico during the World Cup
item.description: Mexico's World Cup run has inspired a phrase heard across the country: "Y si sí." Or in English: "What if we can?" Anamaria Sayre reports from Mexico City.
item.content:encoded: Mexico's World Cup run has inspired a phrase heard across the country: "Y si sí." Or in English: "What if we can?" Anamaria Sayre reports from Mexico City.
item.dc:creator: Anamaria Artemisa Sayre

item.title: NATO leaders look for unity as Trump attends annual summit
item.description: As NATO leaders gather for this week's summit in Turkey, Europe is preparing to shoulder more of its own defense. David M. Cattler of the Center for European Policy Analysis explains why.
item.content:encoded: As NATO leaders gather for this week's summit in Turkey, Europe is preparing to shoulder more of its own defense. David M. Cattler of the Center for European Policy Analysis explains why.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Hong Kong's 'sound of silence': 6 years under the national security law
item.description: Six years after Beijing imposed its National Security Law, many of Hong Kong's opposition voices have gone silent. Danny Vincent looks at what's changed.
item.content:encoded: Six years after Beijing imposed its National Security Law, many of Hong Kong's opposition voices have gone silent. Danny Vincent looks at what's changed.
item.dc:creator: Danny Vincent
